title: Koeleria mannagettae
intro: Koeleria mannagettae, also known as Mannagetta's hair grass, is a perennial grass species belonging to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can help alleviate conditions such as fever, colds, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria mannagettae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Koeleria mannagettae include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, particularly those related to the respiratory and digestive systems.
